{
  "name": "hello-world-express-tailwind",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Minimal Express + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS starter for a Hello World app with a color-editable UI (initialization and deps).",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "ts-node-dev --respawn --transpile-only src/index.ts",
    "build": "tsc",
    "start": "node dist/index.js",
    "build:css": "tailwindcss -i ./src/styles/input.css -o ./public/styles.css --minify",
    "dev:css": "tailwindcss -i ./src/styles/input.css -o ./public/styles.css --watch",
    "test": "jest --passWithNoTests"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "dependencies": {
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.14",
    "express": "^4.18.2",
    "postcss": "^8.4.24",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.7"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^4.17.17",
    "@types/jest": "^29.5.3",
    "@types/supertest": "^2.0.12",
    "jest": "^29.6.1",
    "supertest": "^6.3.3",
    "ts-jest": "^29.1.0",
    "ts-node-dev": "^2.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.2.2"
  }
}

# Hello World Express + Tailwind (Init)

This project initializes an Express + TypeScript server and Tailwind CSS build tooling. It's the starting point for a small app where the frontend UI will later allow changing the "Hello World" text color.

Setup

1. Install dependencies:

   npm install

2. Build Tailwind CSS once (creates public/styles.css):

   npm run build:css

3. For development, run the CSS watcher and server in two terminals:

   npm run dev:css
   npm run dev

4. Build TypeScript for production:

   npm run build
   npm start

Tests

Run automated tests with:

   npm test

Notes

- Tailwind CLI (installed as a dependency) is used to process src/styles/input.css into public/styles.css.
- The Express app serves static files from public/ and exposes a simple GET / endpoint for verification.

Hello World

Use the UI to change the color (UI will be added in later subtasks).

notes: Build Tailwind CSS once with `npm run build:css` to create public/styles.css, or run `npm run dev:css` to watch for changes during development.
Start the TypeScript dev server with `npm run dev`.
Automated tests are provided and can be run with `npm test`.
